entities.name: Kubernetes
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：很难管理的容器管理系统，设计初衷是为缓慢扩展的 Web 服务器，不适合需要频繁上下波动的计算密集型任务，导致工作流编排产品极难用。

evidence.quote: It's because you have to run them on Kubernetes.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: Kubernetes is hard to manage.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: to burst up and down a lot versus like Kubernetes design for like slow scaling
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: which is why would you have an agent read through hundreds of Kubernetes files
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: So there is still a role for the always on large scale Kubernetes type of things.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_01

entities.name: 无服务器 (serverless)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：Modal 构建的基础计算形态，支持任务队列、ETL、突发处理等场景，能够实现真正的按需扩展至零和真正的突发性。

evidence.quote: the serverless container stack that you guys did,

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: 沙箱 (sandbox)

entities.name: GPU 快照 (snapshotting)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：保存 GPU 当前运行状态（如模型状态）的技术，能让下一次调用启动得快得多，也被客户（如 Ramp）用于构建高反应性的会计智能体。

evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: because they were able to use some of the primitives like snapshotting

entities.name: 投机解码 (speculative decoding)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：让小模型（草稿模型）先猜、大模型来验证的加速推理技术；能增加接受长度，带来 2 到 4 倍的乘法性加速且无质量损失。

evidence.quote: how effective speculative decoding really just gets to anything you want
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_00

entities.name: 可观测性 (observability)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：在代码逐渐「黑盒化」的今天极其重要的能力，强调仪表盘和 CLI 工具，因为人类仍然需要去解释系统发生了什么并做出判断。

evidence.quote: One thing we actually still see is really important is observability.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: the rest of our observability like how to something is failing like how do you look
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: 装饰器 (decorator)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：Modal 用于将基础设施的配置需求直接写进代码里的语法，压缩了配置表面积，让用户免于编写大量 YAML，且智能体只需改动几处即可获得自配置运行时。

evidence.quote: in a decorator and it gets this sort of self-provisioning runtime of being able
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: other feature which is you can add a decorator to a function and you get a cluster
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: auto endpoints
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：Modal 推出的开箱即用端点，内置各种优化（如投机解码），提供完全的透明度，用户可拿到代码自己跑，也可随时弹出到完整的 Modal 体验中去微调。

evidence.quote: auto endpoints is we want to make frontier level performance available to everyone
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: to explain what auto endpoints are yeah i love you
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_00

entities.name: DeFlash
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：Modal 开源的一种基于块的推测器，一次预测一整个块而非单个 token，能让开源模型达到专有提供商的性能。

evidence.quote: of recently we shared our work on dflash which is a block based speculator
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: by using open source dflash you can get the same performance as you would with one
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: And so we've been working a bunch on dflash, which is a block based speculator.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: like the dflash stuff already baked in, and there's full transparency.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: modal bench
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：Modal 推出的专门用来测试当前大模型还做不到的那些难事（如读日志排查错误）的基准测试，以此发现智能体的不足并完善产品。

evidence.quote: We're playing around with releasing like a modal bench for like the harder things
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: as I said, we built this benchmark, modal bench, to see where agents are lacking.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: 后训练 (post-training)
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：模型训练的一大趋势，指人们在已有模型基础上做特定优化（如训练中等大小的模型以在推理上获得更高质量），是 Modal 多节点训练的核心适用场景。

evidence.quote: The thing that we've built Multano training for is we see a lot of smaller-scale post-training.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: People are post-training medium-sized fun models so they can get higher quality on inference.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

evidence.quote: That is my impression of how a lot of these labs explore branches in post training
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_01

evidence.quote: with companies that were building their own post training frameworks
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: Suno
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：一家做音频的公司，不使用 Modal 做训练，但因拥有完全自定义的模型架构，将所有推理部署在 Modal 上。

evidence.quote: So we kind of stayed away from the LM space and we were serving companies like Suno

evidence.quote: for audio, Runway for video.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_02

entities.name: Replicate
entities.how_described: 本集把它说成：一个仅提供模型 API 的「倒下的竞争对手」，主持人借此引出 Modal 刻意避开低粘性业余爱好者市场、坚持提供代码级灵活性的话题。

entities.name: Gitpod
